back_translation: On the first day of the second month, the second year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt, the LORD spoke to Moses in the Tent of Meeting, in the wilderness of Sinai, saying:
"You shall count the number of the whole congregation of the Israelites, according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every male, person by person.
From twenty years old and upward, everyone who can go out to war in Israel — you and Aaron shall count them according to their armies.
And let there be one man from each tribe with the two of you; he is the head of the ancestral house of his fathers.
And these are the names of the men who shall stand and assist you: from the tribe of Reuben, Elizur son of Shedeur;
from the tribe of Simeon, Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai;
from the tribe of Judah, Nahshon son of Amminadab;
from the tribe of Issachar, Nethanel son of Zuar;
from the tribe of Zebulun, Eliab son of Helon;
from the sons of Joseph: from the tribe of Ephraim, Elishama son of Ammihud; from the tribe of Manasseh, Gamaliel son of Pedahzur;
from the tribe of Benjamin, Abidan son of Gideoni;
from the tribe of Dan, Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai;
from the tribe of Asher, Pagiel son of Ocran;
from the tribe of Gad, Eliasaph son of Deuel;
and from the tribe of Naphtali, Ahira son of Enan."
These are the ones who were called from the congregation, leaders of the tribes of their ancestors, heads of the clans of Israel.
So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name,

and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation, and recorded their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names from twenty years old and upward, person by person.
Just as the LORD commanded Moses, so he counted them in the wilderness of Sinai.

The sons of Reuben, firstborn of Israel — their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names person by person, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Reuben were 46,500.

From the sons of Simeon, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, those counted of him, by listing the names person by person, every male from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Simeon were 59,300.

From the sons of Gad, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Gad were 45,650.

From the sons of Judah, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Judah were 74,600.

From the sons of Issachar, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Issachar were 54,400.

From the sons of Zebulun, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Zebulun were 57,400.

From the sons of Joseph, that is, from the sons of Ephraim, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Ephraim were 40,500.

From the sons of Manasseh, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Manasseh were 32,200.

From the sons of Benjamin, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Benjamin were 35,400.

From the sons of Dan, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Dan were 62,700.

From the sons of Asher, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Asher were 41,500.

The sons of Naphtali, their genealogies according to their clans, according to their ancestral houses, by listing the names of every man from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war —
those counted from the tribe of Naphtali were 53,400.
These are those who were counted, whom Moses and Aaron and the twelve leaders of Israel counted, one man from each ancestral house.
So those counted from the whole of the Israelites, according to their ancestral houses, from twenty years old and upward, everyone who could go out to war in Israel —
those counted in total were 603,550.
But the Levites, according to the tribe of their ancestors, were not counted among them.
For the LORD had spoken to Moses, saying,
"Do not count the tribe of Levi, and do not lift up their head in the midst of the Israelites.

But you, appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the Testimony, and over all its furnishings, and over everything that belongs to it; they shall carry the tabernacle and all its furnishings, and they shall serve in the tabernacle, and they shall camp around the tabernacle.
When the tabernacle is to set out, the Levites shall take it down; and when the tabernacle is to encamp, the Levites shall set it up; and the outsider who comes near shall be put to death.
The Israelites shall encamp according to their armies, each man in his own camp, and each man under his own standard.

But the Levites shall encamp around the tabernacle of the Testimony, so that wrath shall not be on the congregation of the Israelites; and the Levites shall keep the charge of the tabernacle of the Testimony."
So the Israelites did according to everything that the LORD commanded Moses; thus they did.
